texto: If you leave a bunch of eleven-year-olds to their own devices, what you get is Lord of the Flies. Like a lot of American kids, I read this book in school. Presumably it was not a coincidence. Presumably someone wanted to point out to us that we were savages, and that we had made ourselves a cruel and stupid world. This was too subtle for me. While the book seemed entirely believable, I didn't get the additional message. I wish they had just told us outright that we were savages and our world was stupid.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Paul Graham: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “If you leave a bunch of eleven-year-olds to their own devices, what you get is Lord of the Flies.…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, children, geeks pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de geeks.

While the book seemed entirely believable, I didn't get the additional message. I wish they had just told us outright that we were savages and our world was stupid.

texto: Users are a double-edged sword. They can help you improve your language, but they can also deter you from improving. So choose your users carefully, and be slow to grow their number. Having users is like optimization: the wise course is to delay it.
